observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strengthen the
procedures of review, analysis and reconciliation of the RPT
Receivable/Deferred RPT and the SET Receivable/ Deferred SET
accounts and effect the adjustments/reconciling items in the
respective books to present fairly the balances in the financial
statements.

1.11 The Audit Team further recommended that, for all Real Property Tax
collections, either through cash or check, two RCDs should be prepared
to recognize that SEF and GF shares, respectively, since the RCD will be
the basis of the accounting for recording the transaction.

2.11 We recommended and Management agreed that:
2.11.1 The Municipal Engineer submit a report of all completed
projects with attached Certificate of Completion to the
Accounting Office for recording of these projects to the
appropriate PPE accounts; and
2.11.2 The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting
entry to reclassify the completed projects from the CIP to the
proper PPE accounts and recognize the computed
depreciation or any impairment thereof.

3.8 We recommended and Management agreed that:
3.8.1 The GSO coordinate with other offices to secure records to
locate the PPEs not included in the count, to include the
same in the conduct of physical count, and to prepare and
submit the corresponding RPCPPE within the prescribed
period and reconcile the same with that of the accounting
records;
3.8.2 The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting
entries after reconciling the accounting records and the
result of the physical count of PPEs; and
3.8.3 Comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020, for the one-time cleansing of the PPE
accounts to come up with reconciled balances of the PPE
accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the
agency’s PPE.

4.8 We recommended that:
4.8.1 Management provide a secure area where the unserviceable
properties of the Municipality are stored to ensure that the
properties are safeguarded from natural elements and
possible theft;
4.8.2 The GSO prepare and submit the required Inventory,
Inspection, and Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP)
using the prescribed format and ensuring full compliance
with the guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 92-386. The
timely completion of the inspection and reporting process is
crucial in mitigating potential losses due to asset decay.
Furthermore, the GSO must ensure that the IIRUP for all
identified unserviceable properties is processed and
submitted to the Commission on Audit (COA) for approval in
strict adherence to Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA-DBM
Joint Circular 2024-01; and
4.8.3 Establish a disposal committee in line with the guidelines set
forth in the joint circular. This will streamline the process and
ensure a faster and more efficient disposal of items.

5.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the LSB Secretary
ensure that copies of the SEF Budget and SEF utilization reports are
submitted to the DepEd CO through the proper channels and to the
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer in accordance with Items 5.5
and 6.1 of the DEPED-DBM-DILG J.C. No. 1, s. 2017. The Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer are to base their recording and
certification on the approved SEF budget.

6.9 We recommended that the LFC conduct the necessary review and
assessment of the LEEs and prepare the necessary report in the
manner prescribed by Item 6.6.3 and Section 7 of the LEE Manual in
order to truly evaluate the status of the enterprises and to address
emerging issues and capture opportunities in view of achieving self-
sufficiency and provide additional revenue source for the LGU.

7.8 We recommended that the GAD Focal Person use identified gender
issues as the basis for the PPAs to be included in the GPB.
Moreover, provide adequate documents that will prove that these
are indeed existing and prevalent in the Municipality and use the
HGDG test to prove the gender responsiveness of GAD PPAs, if
applicable.

9.2 We commended the efforts of Management in the proper
withholding and prompt remittance of taxes for CY 2024 and
recommended that this best practice be continued for the
succeeding years.

We recommended and Management agreed for the:
Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Include only checks released to claimants in the RCI;
2. Maintain check register where claimants acknowledge the receipt of the check and indicate date of receipt;
3. Ensure that claimants properly fill up Box E of the disbursement voucher; and
4. Prepare separate report for unreleased checks; and
Municipal Accountant to:
1. Verify the entries in the RCI; and
2. Record only those checks already released to claimants.

Prepare separate report for unreleased checks; and
The office of the Accountant will do a monthly checking of RCI to make sure to only record checks that are released to claimants.
Municipal Accountant to:
1. Verify the entries in the RCI; and
2. Record only those checks already released to claimants.

1.5 We recommended that the GSO plan and conduct the physical count
of all inventories and PPEs and prepare the corresponding RPCI and

municipal accountant is enjoined to prepare and maintain the required
ledger cards for easy monitoring of the account balances and help facilitate
the conduct of the physical inventory.

2.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately
identify the reconciling items of the affected reciprocal accounts and effect
the adjustments thereof in the respective books to present fairly the said
accounts in the financial statements.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the
accounting records with that of the lending institution and effect the
necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the loans payable
account. Also, reclassify the loans amounting to P6,157,152.50 as current
liability to present fairly the account balance in the financial statements.

4.5 We recommended that Management allocate not less than 5% of the
total estimated revenue for regular sources for LDRRMF; Advise the
MDRRMO to include allocation for the response and rehabilitation aspects
of the 70% in the LDRRMFIP and to ensure clear and distinctive allocation
to the four (4) thematic areas of disaster and risk-reduction management as
prescribed in Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and adhere
to the provisions of Section 324(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and Item 4 of NDRRMC-
DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in the creation and allocation of the LDRRMF.

5.4 We recommended that Management execute comprehensible
guidelines/policies or MOA governing financial assistance provided by the
LGU to various groups; Ensure that payments of the funds must be debited
to Due from NGOs/POs or Due from NGAs accounts to maintain
accountability and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No.
1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the grant of financial
aids to various groups and/or institutions.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand
letters to the respective NGOs/POs for the liquidation of the fund transfers.
File a request for write-off of the said accounts in accordance with COA
Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if warranted. Strictly comply
with the requirements of Section 5.3 and 5.4 of COA Circular 2007-001 on
the reporting and liquidation of fund transfers to NGOs/POs.

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit
immediately the lacking transaction documents of CY 2021 and facilitate the
filing and submission of the DVs within the required period. Henceforth,
strictly adhere to Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA
in the submission of accounts.

8.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop granting
RATA to municipal officials and department heads who do not meet the
required actual work performance construed in LBC No. 103 to avoid
payment of illegal expenditure. Strictly adhere to LBC No. 103 in the
granting of RATA to qualified municipal officials and department heads.

9.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send out demand
letters to the contractors for collection/settlement of the unrecouped amount
not deducted from the payments of the progress billings. Strictly adhere to
Section 4.3 Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the grant and
recoupment of advances to contractors.

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enforce the
minimum net take home pay not to lower than P5,000.00 as stated in Section
49 of the GAA in order to promote efficient and effective public service by
ensuring that government employees will have enough to support their basic
needs until the next payday.

11.6 We recommended that the GAD Focal Person support the identified
gender issues contained in the GPB with adequate documents that will prove
that these are indeed existing and prevalent in the Municipality and use the
HGDG test to prove the gender responsiveness of GAD PPAs, if applicable.

strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4.1.c.2.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 in the
preparation and submission of the GPB and GAD AR.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit all paid vouchers promptly to the Accounting Unit in order for the latter to have ample time to prepare the financial reports.  Also, observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGUs to ensure timely submission of financial reports.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct all personnel concerned to submit the required documents for each payment, like the billing, fuel withdrawal slip and driver’s trip ticket; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to require the complete supporting documents prior to payment and record the same directly to expense account to reflect the correct amount of actual FOL incurred during the year and to avoid suspension of transactions.

The Management is enjoined to observe the provisions on the imposition of Liquidated Damages in all its procurement pursuant to the RIRR of RA 9184 in order to safeguard the resources of the government against loss or wastage.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant –Designate be directed to require the concerned payee or the processor of the subject DVs to submit the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation in audit.
Henceforth, any payment of claims should be supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and regularity of transactions otherwise the same shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to locate the missing/lost DVs for submission to the Accounting Unit, otherwise she shall be held liable therefor. Also, instruct her to establish an appropriate control measures in the Treasury Office to ensure that all paid DVs will be returned immediately to the Accounting Unit after payment for proper recording and submission to COA as prescribed in COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere to its mandate which is to determine if the suppliers are reputable and qualified to transact with the government and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of Republic Act 9184 and its revised implementing rules and regulations in all procurement of goods, services and infrastructures, to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditure, as provided in the above cited COA circular.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management cause the refund of the total amount of Php2,987,530.00 from Naliyagan Stakeholders Foundation Incorporated, otherwise a notice of disallowance will be issued for the said transaction. Observe strictly the provisions of Section 88 of PD 1445 to avoid incurrence of irregular disbursements.
The Management has not commented on this audit finding.
We recommended that the concerned officers be directed to refund the total amount of Php4,824.23 and instruct them to pay the loans on or before the due date to avoid penalties and charges.

We recommended that Management stop the payment of PERA to concerned contract of service/JO employees pursuant to DBM Budget Circular No. 2009-3, dated August 18, 2009 and CSC Resolution No. Budget Circular No. 020790, dated June 5, 2002, to avoid incurrence of irregular disbursements.
Management committed to adhere to the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management direct the MAgO to monitor the actions taken by the Cooperative in addressing the current issue on high cost of power bill and ensure its immediate resolution so that the PO can maximize the utilization of the equipment as well as the materials and other resources given to cooperative.
Likewise, instruct the MPDC to be prudent in the preparation of the programs and plans for each project taking into consideration all aspects that would affect the implementation and utilization of the project to ensure its optimal use and to prevent wastage of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Peace and Order Council revisit the provisions of EO No. 309, series of 1987 to understand fully their duties and functions in order to generate more viable PPAs and a reliable peace and order plan and budget.

We recommended that Management ensure that the existing economic enterprise of the LGU can sustain its operating expenses and should not rely on the subsidy from the General Fund, otherwise consider the dissolution of the same.

We recommended that the Local School Board plan thoroughly the SEF budget for the year and submit the same promptly to the implementing office to avoid delay or non-implementation of the identified priority projects and activities. Likewise, regular monitoring and reporting on the implementation should be adopted in order to address immediately the reasons for non-implementation and effect any revision to the budget, if warranted, to ensure optimal utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to monitor closely the utilization of the raw materials already issued to AWMUPCO to ensure that these will not be put into wastage. Require the AWMUPCO to submit reports on the production of the feeds and the utilization thereof.
Require the concerned implementing office to fast track the implementation of the BuB projects to provide timely benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that Management instruct the GAD Focal Point to prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied with its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for validation and tracking the resources of gender responsive and/or GAD focused policies, programs and projects based on the approved GAD plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to institute measures to ensure that all withheld taxes during the year will be recorded in the books on time and remitted in full to the BIR within the prescribed period.
